meaning: v.收回,恢复,重到,重新夺得
examples: I struggled to regain some dignity.
She paused on the edge, trying to regain her balance.
She will now begin occupational therapy to regain the use of her hands.
For a newspaper to regain its business standing, it has to regain its community.
Ultimately, the success of his initiatives will determine whether majorities regain confidence in government.
